titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Stephen John Keynes Collection Files
titlestmt.author: Michaela Gibbons

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 30, 2025 at 23:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Stephen John Keynes collection files shed light on the process of manuscript collecting in the 1970s through 1990s. The collection contains catalogues of manuscripts and miniatures as well as correspondence, invoices, and notes related to items of Keynes' business interest.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Stephen John Keynes collection files shed light on the process of manuscript collecting in the 1970s through 1990s. The collection contains catalogues of manuscripts and miniatures as well as correspondence, invoices, and notes related to items of Keynes' business interest. The collected catalogues were published between 1973 and 2001 by household names such as Christie's, Sotheby's, and Maggs Bros. Ltd, and offer insight into the identity, condition, and demand of artifacts that informed Keynes' purchasing decisions. With the catalogues are notes written by Keynes that were either tucked into specific pages or found inside the cover. Several boxes in this collection contain books from the Sotheby's catalogue series Western Manuscripts and Miniatures, which describes artifacts between the ninth and nineteenth century. Papers related to the acquisition of manuscripts include correspondence with antiquarian booksellers regarding location and pricing. Keynes' professional relationships with these sellers stretched over decades. Many manuscripts referenced have annotated catalogue copies and invoices detailing their history and value. Lastly, this collection contains three artifacts obtained by Keynes that are partially identified by their short description and item number.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Catalogues, 1973-2001; II. Manuscripts, 1804-2017; III. Professional papers, 1980-1995.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection files were removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Richard B. Linenthal on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Stephen John Keynes collection files. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stephen John Keynes was born on October 19, 1929, in London, United Kingdon. He was the fourth son of surgeon and bibliophile Geoffrey Langdon Keynes (1887-1982) and Margaret Elizabeth Darwin (1890-1975). Keynes's extended family included his great-grandfather Charles Darwin and uncle John Maynard Keynes. He attended both public and private schools before becoming an Open Scholar at King's College, Cambridge, where he participated in the arts.

After graduating, he did National Service with the Royal Artillery until 1951 when he began a career as a merchant banker. In 1955, Keynes married Mary Cecilia Knatchbull-Hugessen, the daughter of a Canadian senator. They proceeded to have five children in six years. Knatchbull-Hugessen later became a teacher in London.

After twenty years of banking, Keynes turned to book collecting. In 1971, he began his second career at a community bookshop, which was one of the few businesses in town welcoming to LGBTQ+ individuals, people of color, and low-income families. Keynes became a member of the Roxburghe Club in 1978, the world's oldest bibliographic and publishing society, of which his father and, later, brother were members. The bulk of his collecting occurred between the 1970s and 1980s. He specialized in Western artifacts from the Middle Ages. While many of his books were acquired through antiquarian booksellers, some of his collection was inherited from his parents. His mother left him a collection of autographed letters and manuscripts assembled over the course of her lifetime, which included materials from Edwardian and Victorian political figures and members of the British Royal family. With his brother Quentin Keynes, he founded the Charles Darwin Trust in 1999.
Keynes continued his collecting work until his death on August 13, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by publisher with associated material filed after its corresponding catalogue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes antiquarian catalogues and related material tucked into specific pages or inside the cover by Keynes.

title.part: The Mostyn Hall Library: Printed Books and Manuscripts Part III: P-Z
Text Manuscripts and Documents 2200 BC to 1600 AD
The Celebrated Library of the Late Major J.R. Abbey: The Ninth Portion: The Hornby Manuscripts, Part II
Illuminated Manuscripts from the Celebrated Library of the Late Major J.R. Abbey: The Eleventh and Final Part
Fine Books and manuscripts from the Library of the late Alan G. Thomas
Medieval Illuminated Miniatures from the Collection of the Late Eric Korner
Phillipps: Medieval Manuscripts: Part VIII: Catalogue of Manuscripts on Papyrus, Vellum, and Paper
Phillipps: Medieval Manuscripts: Part X: Catalogue of Manuscripts on Papyrus, Vellum, and Paper
Phillipps: Medieval Manuscripts: Part XI: Catalogue of Manuscripts on Papyrus, Vellum, and Paper
Single Leaves & Miniatures from Western Illuminated Manuscripts
Western and Hebrew Manuscripts and Miniatures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, correspondence, invoices, and catalogue copies.

title.part: A Book of Hours from the Limbourg Atelier
Bound for the Earl of Bridgewater
Breviary for a Dominican Nun of Poissy
Circle of the Duc de Berry
The DeLivis Hours and the Bedford Workshop

did.unittitle: European Miniatures and Illuminations catalogue, catalogue copies, correspondence, and notes

title.part: A Gospel Lectionary from the Foundation Period of Portugal

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The contents of this folder are related to Folder 40 but remain separate as pre-arranged by Keynes.

title.part: Master of the Fransican Breviary of Bologna

did.unittitle: Siya Kniga Sobryanie Sloves' I Deyanii , 1820
Catalogue copy, correspondence, invoices, and notes
Item 6278, "16th c. English Living Note of Ownership"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials sent to Stephen Keynes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Referencing event details and preservation and printing practices.
